channel.title: Twine on Riibaisu
channel.description: Recent content in Twine on Riibaisu

item.title: My First Experience with Twine
item.description:
What is Twine?

In its website, Twine is defined as “an open-source tool for telling interactive, nonlinear stories”. I like to think about it as a branching dialog game creator.

My Experience

After finishing the [[Customer Cat Postmortem|last game jam]], I was eager to participate in another one, but I only had 8 hours to submit something. With my current skill set, I knew I couldn’t make a polished graphical game in that time, so I decided to write an interactive story instead. It’s a game after all, right?

item.title: Espiral
item.description:
Espiral means “spiral” in Portuguese.

Espiral is a game I made in Twine for the Last 3 Minutes Jam about a anxious person having to face their interview in the next three minutes.

It has 3 endings, but just 2 are actually very different. The main theme is anxiety and low self-esteem, and how that triggers overthinking to the point of panic and giving up.
